reasoning: Qin Haiyang currently holds the 200m breaststroke long course world record at 2:05.48, set in July 2023. The 2025 World Aquatics Championships effectively ended today (August 3, 2025), with Qin himself winning the 200m breast in 2:07.41, missing his own record by two seconds and no one else breaking it. Looking ahead from August 2025 to January 2026, the international swimming calendar shifts almost entirely to short course meters (25m), including the World Aquatics Swimming World Cup and the Short Course World Championships. Major long course competitions are extremely rare during this period, and elite swimmers typically do not taper for long course world records in the fall or winter months. The only real threat to his standing as the record holder would be a retroactive doping ban, but despite ongoing scrutiny over CHINADA and WADA, any disciplinary action would likely involve a protracted legal process well past January 2026. As a result, the probability of him losing the record via a faster swim or doping disqualification on these dates is very low, slowly increasing over time to account for unforeseen developments.
